title: Slow Clients, Resource Limits, Backpressure, Admission, and Cleanup · Execution Trace: Slow Header Body Defense
summary: A server gives every connection a goroutine and unlimited buffer, refreshes deadlines on single-byte trickles, queues output without bounds, and retries accept failures until the process collapses. Planned artifact: Add and verify the slow header body defense increment in connection resource budget and overload policy. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Bound start-line, header, content, trailer, idle, and total-phase time using both progress and absolute budgets.
Test HTTP/2 or HTTP/3 to HTTP/1.1 translation, proxy chains, cache parsing, and backend normalization with the same adversarial corpus.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Predict Slow Header Body Defense · Theory Slow Header Body Defense
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory protocol team handles case 4120 in a municipal gateway receiving one-byte TCP fragments. Build deterministic Go evidence to combine absolute phase time, memory, and output-queue budgets for slow-peer defense; preserve this operating constraint: all attacker-controlled counts must be validated before allocation or slicing; then block extra QPACK streams and fall back without removing HTTP/2 availability. Browser code models bounded byte and state decisions only. Sockets, listeners, live TLS, proxies, packet capture, fuzz campaigns, races, load, HTTP/2, HTTP/3, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates. Competency probe: Bound start-line, header, content, trailer, idle, and total-phase time using both progress and absolute budgets.
